plainEnglish: Actions should fire on the up-event (release), not the down-event (press), so a user who presses by mistake can slide off the target to cancel. Alternatively, provide undo. This lets people with motor difficulties recover from accidental presses.
whyItMatters: Users with motor disabilities who press a control by accident can't cancel the action if it fires immediately on the down-press instead of on release.
quickCheck: Confirm actions trigger on the click/up-event (not mousedown/touchstart) so users can drag off to abort, or that an undo is available.
commonMistakes: Triggering actions on onMouseDown or onTouchStart instead of onClick (the up-event).
Providing no way to abort an action by moving off the control before releasing.
Drag actions that commit on the down-event with no undo.

codeExamples.label: Fires on release, so it can be aborted
codeExamples.code:
{/* onClick fires on the up-event; slide off to cancel */}
<button onClick={deleteItem}>Delete</button>

codeExamples.label: Fires on press with no chance to cancel
codeExamples.code:
{/* Commits the instant it is pressed */}
<button onMouseDown={deleteItem}>Delete</button>
